name: Irish Brown Bread Royal Hibernian
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the whole wheat flour, sugar, baking soda, and salt.
2. In a separate bowl, beat the eggs until frothy and add buttermilk. Combine the milk mixture with softened butter.
3. Mix in the milk mixture with the flour mixture using an electric mixer for 2 minutes.
4. Gradually add white flour and mix thoroughly.
5. Divide the dough into two and shape into round balls.
6. Flatten the tops slightly and cut an "X" about 1/2 inch deep on each loaf.
7. Place the loaves on a greased cookie sheet or into two 9x5-inch loaf pans (for loaf pans, cut a slash down the center lengthwise).
8. Bake at 400°F for 50 minutes.
9. Cool before cutting into thin slices.
